title: bug: --userns=auto does not work for CLI nor podman-remote for more than 1 ephemeral containtainer
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
` --userns=auto` does not work for CLI nor podman-remote for more than 1 ephemeral containtainer

Related: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15476 @flouthoc 

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
sudo su

cat << EOF > /etc/subuid
containers:2147483647:2147483648
EOF

cat << EOF > /etc/subgid
containers:2147483647:2147483648
EOF

cat << EOF > Dockerfile
FROM alpine
RUN echo foo
RUN echo bar
EOF

podman -r build -t foobar --no-cache --userns=auto .
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@ip-10-2-20-212 test]# podman -r build -t test --no-cache --userns=auto .
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
STEP 2/3: RUN echo fo
fo
--> c6bb757f3b9
time="2022-10-31T16:51:02Z" level=error msg="error deleting build container \"7b897b1a077aaecfb44d10cc0df82cd3723b00f95b439a057975a55c4c0e68c7\": identifier is not a container\n"
Error: identifier is not a container: preparing container for next step: creating build container: creating container: the container needs a user namespace with size '�' that is bigger than the maximum va
lue allowed with userns=auto '𐀀'
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman image

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
4.3.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

  cpuUtilization:

  cpus: 16
  distribution:

  idMappings:
    gidmap: null

  kernel: 6.0.9-300.fc37.x86_64

  ociRuntime:

      password_hash: $y$j9T$rDwS28ZvFa0MWg4/3/fLt0$Fq44tpDJBxfEs9lsmKMgq.AYI/Rw/g3W5x.qf9/U3d0

    - path: /etc/subgid

          test:165536:65536
          containers:100000:65536
```
@lukasmrtvy I'll see if i can reproduce this on exact aws env but as of now I am unable to reproduce this on `f37` and `f35` and maybe that's why it passes in CI too.
@lukasmrtvy Can you apply above PR in your env and paste error message again ?

do you have other containers?  When you use `--userns=auto` it picks a range that is not used, but if you have other containers that you've created automatically then there are probably no free ranges left

@giuseppe  no, see the reproducer https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16795#issuecomment-1346326070 , no containers, no ephemeral containers running

EDIT: it does not work explicitly for the **root** user

you are using podman remote, what is the connection string?  How is the server configured?

You may need to restart the podman server to reload the mappings
It does not work for CLI, even for podman-remote. Mapping does work for podman container create --userns=auto ( without restart ). Is there any major difference for buildah? Thanks
I'll try reproducing with `root` actually my last reproduce attempt was only with `rootless`, I'll verify If i'm able to reproduce this for build containers :)

Hi @lukasmrtvy , I don't exactly know the limit in fcos but I think smaller range can work for both rootless and rootfull there should be no need to do such big allocations on the edge anyways. You can use `--userns=auto:size=<somesize>` to set the limit.

So this works for me both `rootless` and `rootful`, I think this should work for you as well.

```console
[core@localhost ~]$ cat /etc/subuid
containers:1000:5000
core:6001:1000
[core@localhost ~]$ cat /etc/subgid
containers:1000:5000
core:6001:1000
##
## Rootless
##
[core@localhost ~]$ podman build --userns=auto:size=500 --no-cache .
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
STEP 2/3: RUN echo a
a
--> aee21357828
STEP 3/3: RUN echo b
b
COMMIT
--> 6e00fdf4dd0
6e00fdf4dd0bd515d8ff855189d3c051a60f3c7328de153e06459ee56d6f4a71
##
## Rootful
##
[core@localhost ~]$ sudo su -
[root@localhost ~]# podman build --userns=auto --no-cache .
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
STEP 2/3: RUN echo a
a
--> 199c914b568
STEP 3/3: RUN echo b
b
COMMIT
--> d325cd5cacd
d325cd5cacd90244cbc37df0f7fed75b62ae0ec0d861527e2a897eaec14c0e67
[root@localhost ~]# 
```

There is a lot of fuzz in this issue, but if I am not mistaken, max *UID is defined in /etc/login.defs. So theoreticaly:

 ```
cat /etc/login.defs | grep ID_MAX
# No LASTLOG_UID_MAX means that there is no user ID limit for writing
#LASTLOG_UID_MAX
UID_MAX                 60000
SYS_UID_MAX               999
SUB_UID_MAX             600100000
GID_MAX                 60000
SYS_GID_MAX               999
SUB_GID_MAX             6001000
```

```
cat /etc/subuid
core:100000:65536
containers:200000:599900000
```
```
cat /etc/subgid
core:100000:65536
containers:200000:599900000
```
```
cat /etc/containers/storage.conf 
[storage.options]
auto-userns-min-size=200000
auto-userns-max-size=600100000
```
should be the correct settings then. Hope someone would confirm this. Thanks

> Can't you modify the following field in storage.conf?
> 
> ```
> # auto-userns-max-size=65536
> ```
@rhatdan 
This is working for 4.3.1, see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16795#issuecomment-1379058745, but it does not work with 4.4.0-dev. Currently trying podman:101:0.0.git.17648.1e451031-1 package from https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next to test another bugfix https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/8d8d6be5af0e2b46842610ea404a3e0cf22770b1, but `auto-userns-max-size=600100000` in storage.conf is completely ignored. Is there a regression? Thanks
@giuseppe PTAL ^^

```
cat /etc/containers/storage.conf 
[storage.options]
auto-userns-min-size=200000
auto-userns-max-size=600100000
```

these define the maximum size of the user namespace for a single pod/container.

I don't think you want to allocate all your available IDs to a single one.  I'd suggest something like:

```
cat /etc/containers/storage.conf 
[storage.options]
auto-userns-min-size=1024
auto-userns-max-size=65536
```

@giuseppe are You sure? I dont know, but for 4.3.1:

```
[root@ip-10-2-2-179 core]# cat /etc/sub*id
core:100000:65536
containers:200000:599900000
core:100000:65536
containers:200000:599900000
[root@ip-10-2-2-179 core]# cat /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
auto-userns-min-size=200000
auto-userns-max-size=600100000
[root@ip-10-2-2-179 core]# podman build --userns=auto --no-cache .
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
STEP 2/3: RUN echo a
a
--> 0167dc1ea58
STEP 3/3: RUN echo b
b
COMMIT
--> 80b0db01eb1
80b0db01eb1877884d760fff5be2c34195bf7593cd8c072008e629161c9ddc73
```

Without `auto-userns-min-size` and `auto-userns-max-size` specified, fallbacks to `1024` and `65536` probably
```
[root@ip-10-2-2-179 core]# cat /etc/sub*id
core:100000:65536
containers:200000:599900000
core:100000:65536
containers:200000:599900000
[root@ip-10-2-2-179 core]# cat /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
#[storage.options]
#auto-userns-min-size=200000
#auto-userns-max-size=600100000
[root@ip-10-2-2-179 core]# podman build --userns=auto --no-cache .
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
STEP 2/3: RUN echo a
a
--> 996958c1d63
ERRO[0000] error deleting build container "5a195526d22f9b4a6b603df87a070434081fa695fb3def801abf71c152e9df6c": identifier is not a container
Error: identifier is not a container: preparing container for next step: creating build container: creating container: the container needs a user namespace with size '𰵁' that is bigger than the maximum value allowed with userns=auto '𐀀'
```

AFAIK `containers:200000:599900000` gives 200000-600100000 range and `auto-userns-min-size` and `auto-userns-max-size` to me looks like min and max values for a range that can Podman use.

@giuseppe I also pointed to a problem where storage.conf values at least in `storage.options` table are not reflected at all ( I tried to remove `driver` from `storage` table for each podman version to see if even storage.conf file is reflected and it is, btw )...

4.3.1
```
[root@ip-10-2-1-241 core]# cat /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
root-auto-userns-user="onedoesnotexist"
[root@ip-10-2-1-241 core]# podman build --userns=auto --no-cache .

ERRO[0000] Cannot find mappings for user "onedoesnotexist": no subuid ranges found for user "onedoesnotexist" in /etc/subuid
Error: creating build container: creating container: could not find enough available IDs
```

opened a PR for c/storage: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1471

if you force the config file like `CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF=/etc/containers/storage.conf` then all the values are used, while if it is used because it is the default config file we cherry-pick some of the settings, just the ones that are meant to work with rootless.

Could you try if the patch proposed for containers/storage fixes the problem you're seeing?
